subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Medieval Monks and Healing
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Medieval Monks and Healing.

hook.text: When Rome fell, monasteries became the only hospitals in Europe for 500 years.

buildup.text: Monks preserved Greek and Roman medical texts, copying them by hand in candlelight.

discovery.text: They created herbal gardens to grow medicines and treated travelers and the poor.

twist.text: The Church banned monks from surgery in 1215, pushing that work to barbers instead.

climax.text: Barber-surgeons became Europe's first practical first-aiders for centuries.

punchline.text: Your barber's red-and-white pole? It once meant blood and bandages.

quiz.question: Why did barber-surgeons become common in medieval Europe?
options: The Church banned monks from performing surgery
Monks refused to treat common people
